title: Rich Ethiopian Matcha Stew
description: A sweet, chewy, paleo-friendly stew that blends fragrant Ethiopian spices with earthy matcha, simmered with sweet potatoes and coconut milk for a creamy, velvety finish.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat and sauté the diced onion until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and ginger and cook for another 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in the berbere spice and ground cinnamon, allowing them to toast briefly, about 1 minute.
Pour in the chicken broth and coconut milk, then bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Add the diced sweet potatoes and matcha powder, stirring until the matcha has dissolved completely.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and let the stew simmer for 20 minutes, or until the sweet potatoes are tender.
Stir in the honey and season with salt to taste, adjusting the consistency if needed.
Serve the stew hot, optionally garnished with fresh cilantro or chopped nuts for extra crunch.
Enjoy this comforting, high-fat Ethiopian matcha stew that balances sweet, spice, and earthy flavors in a single pot.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
